title: rubus fruticosus subsp. foliosus
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. foliosus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a subspecies of the blackberry plant native to North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus subsp. foliosus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, and exhibit potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, are commonly used to treat various health issues, including wounds, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
